recent_candidates.title: Playbook Special Edition: Meet Our New Authors
recent_candidates.excerpt: The unofficial guide to official Washington. Jul 19, 2026 View in browser By Adam Wren, Dasha Burns and Will Steakin Big news this evening: Playbook is getting a new team. Beginning tomorrow, Playbook will be co-authored by ADAM WREN, DASHA BURNS and WILL STEAKIN — three of the sharpest reporters in American politics, taking the wheel at exactly the moment when stakes are the highest. A midterm year full of surprises, from President Donald Trump’s endorsements to a nationwide left-wing challenge toppling establishment Democrats. A wide-open 2028 race already taking shape. And a battle over the future of both parties that grows more intense by the day. They’ll be working over the next several weeks toward an official relaunch on August 31, when you can expect a newsletter with more reporting, deeper sourcing and an even sharper read on where power is shifting — from the campaign trail to Capitol Hill to the West Wing. What won't change: Playbook will still be the indispensable morning briefing you start your day with. The Playbook Podcast is also getting a refresh — anchored by DASHA, with a rotating cast of her fellow authors and POLITICO journalists breaking down the biggest stori

recent_candidates.title: Iran’s Southern Front
recent_candidates.excerpt: Today in The New York Review of Books: Amir Ahmadi Arian focuses on the south of Iran, currently bearing the brunt of Trump’s war; Aryeh Neier defends the International Criminal Court; Geoffrey O’Brien watches Spielberg’s latest; and, from the archives, Geoffrey O’Brien on Minority Report. Amir Ahmadi Arian The War of the South [https://www.nybooks.com/online/2026/07/19/the-war-of-the-south-iran/] For most of its modern history, the south of Iran has been subject to two forms of colonialism, one from the land and the other from the sea. Aryeh Neier ‘So-Called International Law’ [https://www.nybooks.com/online/2026/07/18/so-called-international-law-trump-icc/] The US has long held the International Criminal Court in suspicion—but never before has an administration tried to destroy it altogether. Geoffrey O’Brien They Knew Too Much [https://www.nybooks.com/online/2026/07/14/they-knew-too-much-disclosure-day/] Steven Spielberg’s new film is a discordant, ambiguous errand into the maze of ufology. recent_candidates.title: Who Cares about the Odyssey?
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://www.raweggstack.com/p/who-cares-about-the-odyssey I promised myself I would not write about The Odyssey. “Charles,” that little voice in my head began to say, “there is absolutely nothing of value to be written about that cursed film, certainly not in an opinion piece. Enough ink has already been spilled, enough Tweets have already been Tweeted, including paid sponsorships dispensed from a hasbara fund so deep it would make even our man in Tel Aviv swoon from vertigo. Just leave it be.” After a brief pause, the homunculus piloting my meat-suit continued: “Remember: You’ve posted the hilarious memes and called her Helen of Detroit—for which you received furious copyright strikes from the film studio—and you’ve also offered compelling analysis of why the film is not a ‘Trojan Horse” expertly crafted to smuggle an anti-woke message into the very heart of Hollywood’s wokest of wokes. People can figure out for themselves that a guy like Jonathan Pageau—the sort of theory-obsessed blowhard who can’t read a menu without pontificating about the symbolism of the entrees in Leviticus—is just talking out of his French-Canadian trou du cul. (Yes, you could

recent_candidates.title: Ep. 4: In the World, Not of It
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://catholicmanhood.substack.com/p/ep-4-in-the-world-not-of-it What does it actually mean to be in the world but not of it? In this meditation, Nick walks through the Letter to Diognetus, a second-century document written to a pagan who wanted to understand what Christians were made of. The portrait it paints is of men fully present in the world and made of completely different material. Citizens who were also aliens. Men whose lives required an explanation. The question it puts to every Catholic man: Does your life require an explanation? Companion Article In the World. Not of It. — [ https://substack.com/redirect/4fa714d1-618e-40ce-a08d-cab891452cb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ]catholicmanhood.substack.com [ https://substack.com/redirect/9f8e539e-e4e0-4f09-807d-cd1790491e84?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] Credits Host: Nick Sledge Producer: John Osterhoudt Follow Catholic Manhood on Social Media X [ https://substack.com/redirect/f1aabe0c-0bd1-49e4-a6c5-a1aa04a65eae?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] FB [ https://substack.com/redirect/7be53267-2c

recent_candidates.title: New Article: Wiring Capital to Compute
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://letter.palladiummag.com/p/new-article-wiring-capital-to-compute This article by Daniel Painter was published at Palladium Magazine [ https://substack.com/redirect/2c620a62-b931-4a27-8482-4f3b72347c26?j=eyJ1IjoiMmpwdzcifQ.3xfUjhbxmXCecLGnlb_rhBXs-3STnTomTjes1rmBMMI ] on July 20, 2026. Over a thousand acres of land in Abilene, Texas, lie flattened with graded earth and poured concrete. The foundation pits are filled with conduit bundles and switchgear, and in some quadrants, fully operational substations have been erected. However, other quadrants sit empty, waiting on a schedule that keeps sliding to the right. Although the supercomputer intended for the site is humming with activity, what registers to a visitor is that the campus is only half-alive. Abilene was the intended flagship of the Stargate consortium. Announced from the White House days after President Trump’s 2025 inauguration, SoftBank’s Masayoshi Son, Oracle’s Larry Ellison, and OpenAI’s Sam Altman stood beside the president as he declared the project’s ambition to pour $500 billion into a combined data center and supercomputer over a four-year [ https://substack.com/redirect/a972555

recent_candidates.title: Short Take: Was “The Odyssey” about Embezzlement?
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://www.anarchonomicon.com/p/short-take-was-the-odyssey-about Was “The Odyssey” about Embezzlement? HELLO. This is insane. So Nolan is getting 15% off direct Revenue, (Not profits, Gross) and his wife another 5%) from the Odyssey, basically risk free, AFTER his 20 million up front. That’s a better cut than a lot of mob bosses take off drug dealers. (which tend to vary between 5 and 30% depending on how much violence they can command) And probably core stars (Daiman and Hathaway mostly) are getting some smaller percent of the same deal. This is unheard of. Basically a violation of fiduciary duties to the investors. Studio execs paid all these people to make this movie this way against their own investors interests. So if another 10-30% of Gross is divided across Daiman, Hathaway, Holand, Pattinson, and Theron... You know the real stars (Zendaya, Nyong’o, and Page don’t draw crowds and aren’t getting that)... Then you might have to effectively cut all revenue first by 50% for theaters, then by another 40-50% for the star power. It other words... The Odyssey would need to make 800million- 1 billion before it could even hypothetically start to turn a pr

recent_candidates.excerpt: No Images? Click here https://fdd-new.cmail19.com/t/r-e-tutkyull-oophjwr-g/ July 20, 2026 | This Is Beirut As the Shia Crescent Wanes, Aoun Stakes Lebanon’s Future on Washington [https://www.fdd.org/analysis/2026/07/20/as-the-shia-crescent-wanes-aoun-stakes-lebanons-future-on-washington/] Hussain Abdul-Hussain [https://www.fdd.org/team/hussain-abdul-hussain/] | Research Fellow Lebanese President Joseph Aoun in Beirut January 9, 2025. (ANWAR AMRO/AFP via Getty Images) As Iran’s once-expanding “Shia Crescent” of influence across Iraq, Syria, Lebanon, and Yemen wanes, a new American-led regional order is emerging. Ahead of his first visit to the White House, Lebanese President Joseph Aoun has clearly recognized this new reality. “It is my duty to seize all opportunities that come to us with solutions,” Aoun said [https://www.nidaalwatan.com/article/390090-%D8%B9%D9%88%D9%86-%D8%A7%D9%84%D8%B3%D9%84%D8%B7%D8%A9-%D8%A7%D9%84%D8%B3%D9%8A%D8%A7%D8%B3%D9%8A%D8%A9-%D9%88%D9%82%D8%A7%D8%A6%D8%AF-%D8%A7%D9%84%D8%AC%D9%8A%D8%B4-%D8%B9%D9%84%D9%89-%D9%82%D9%84%D8%A8-%D9%88%D8%A7%D8%AD%D8%AF] in a July 11 interview. “And today there is a golden opportunity called America that we must seize.” The

recent_candidates.title: Were There Many Nonwhites in Bronze Age Greece?
recent_candidates.excerpt: View this post on the web at https://www.stevesailer.net/p/were-there-many-nonwhites-in-bronze A curious feature of Christopher Nolan’s hit movie The Odyssey is that Bronze Age Greece in 1200 BC is portrayed as having about the same racial demographics as an upscale Los Angeles private school in 2026 where Nolan would send his kids: mostly white and the rest a random mixture of East Asians, South Asians, Sub-Saharans, and even one Latino. Demographically, the cast of The Odyssey is much like that seen in a high school musical production of My Fair Lady. (It even has a girl playing a boy.) Likewise, the casting of The Odyssey seems slightly amateurish. It’s not at all uncommon in adventure epics to portray characters from far away, such as T.E. Lawrence in Lawrence of Arabia, Gurkha soldier Billy Fish in The Man Who Would Be King, and Azeem in the Kevin Costner Robin Hood. But these kind of movies traditionally took pains to offer an explanation for why, say, Morgan Freeman is in medieval Sherwood Forest. The backstories of how they got there are part of the adventure. But The Odyssey is very different in that it doesn’t deign to offer any explanations for its distracting casting. L

recent_candidates.title: Corruption Takes Center Stage in Ukraine’s National Conversation
recent_candidates.excerpt: Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary and analysis from across the political spectrum only for War on the Rocks members. Each Monday, we bring you a curated selection of articles from Ukrainian media offering insight into how Ukrainians themselves debate the issues shaping their country.American coverage often narrows the view to the battlefield — these pieces widen it, revealing the texture of daily life, politics, and public argument in a nation at war. The perspectives gathered here are varied, candid, and often surprising, together forming a more complete picture of Ukraine as it really is.Frontline and StrategyУкраїнська правда The post Corruption Takes Center Stage in Ukraine’s National Conversation appeared first on War on the Rocks .
